# Reconstruction

The fixture asks when a payoff comparison uniquely determines a static equilibrium action. Strict payoff ranking selects one action, but equality leaves both actions optimal. The headline proposition extends uniqueness to the equality boundary without a tie-breaking rule. There are no staged interventions, related-result edges, omitted motivated objects, or quantitative magnitudes. The strongest supported result is uniqueness away from equality and a set-valued optimum at equality.
